bibliographic_info.title: High-precision services of BeiDou navigation satellite system (BDS): current state, achievements, and future directions

grounding_quotes: The operational PPP-B2b of BDS-3 achieves positioning accuracy at decimeter-level within 14 min and has the drawbacks in regional coverage and long convergence time compared with the international counterparts, such as CLAS of QZSS and HAS of Galileo.

Keywords BeiDou navigation satellite system, Precise point positioning, PPP-B2b, Low-earth-orbit, PPP-RTK, PPP-AR, Orbit, Clock Introduction As a Chinese fundamental facility in space and timing information transfer, the development of Beidou Satel- *Correspondence: lite Navigation System (BDS) follows three steps.

To provide more accurate correc- lite orbit, clock, DCB, and UPD parameters of GPS L1/ tions over broader areas (Hu et al., 2003), CORSnet-NSW L5/L2C signals and Galileo E1/E5b/E5a/E6/E5 signals began providing Continuously Operating Reference worldwide through E6 signal of MEO satellites (EUSpace, Stations (CORS) services in Sydney, New South Wales 2023).
